seeded_hokkaido_ramen_santouka.text: This ramen features incredibly tender pork cheek (toroniku) that melts in your mouth, served with a delicate yet flavorful shio broth. It's a truly luxurious and iconic ramen experience that sets a high bar.

seeded_ramen_danbo.text: Danbo's Tonkotsu is celebrated for its rich, creamy pork broth and highly customizable options for noodle firmness, broth richness, and spice level. This allows every diner to craft their perfect, deeply satisfying bowl.

seeded_ooink.text: Ooink's Spicy Shoyu Ramen is a flavor explosion, known for its incredibly rich broth often enhanced with a generous layer of pork back fat and a customizable spice kick. It's a bold and deeply satisfying bowl that truly stands out.

seeded_kizuki_ramen_izakaya.text: The Garlic Tonkotsu at Kizuki offers a robust and savory pork broth elevated by a fragrant garlic oil, adding an irresistible aromatic depth. It's a comforting and flavorful choice for those who love a strong garlic presence.

seeded_betsutenjin_ramen.text: Betsutenjin brings authentic Hakata-style ramen to Seattle with its incredibly rich, milky white pork bone broth and thin, firm noodles. This bowl is a true taste of Fukuoka, perfect for purists seeking a classic experience.

seeded_arashi_ramen.text: This ramen takes a classic Tonkotsu broth and infuses it with fragrant black garlic oil (mayu), creating a distinctively nutty and complex flavor profile. It's a popular choice for those seeking a unique twist on a beloved classic.

seeded_jinya_ramen_bar.text: Jinya's vegan ramen is a standout, offering a surprisingly rich and creamy vegetable broth with a delightful spicy kick, proving that plant-based ramen can be incredibly satisfying. It's a fantastic option that doesn't compromise on flavor or texture.

seeded_samurai_noodle.text: Samurai Noodle's Tonkotsu Ramen is a long-standing favorite, offering a consistently solid and comforting pork bone broth that hits all the right notes. It's a reliable choice for a classic and satisfying ramen experience.

seeded_menya_musashi.text: Menya Musashi's White Tsukemen features thick, chewy noodles served separately from a rich, flavorful dipping broth, allowing for a unique and interactive eating experience. The broth is deeply savory, clinging perfectly to each noodle for an unforgettable bite.

seeded_sushi_kashiba.text: Master Shiro Kashiba's artistry shines in this traditional Edomae-style omakase, featuring pristine, perfectly prepared seasonal fish. Each piece is a delicate balance of flavor and texture, showcasing decades of expertise.

The Hokkaido Uni Nigiri at Sushi Kashiba is an unparalleled experience, featuring incredibly fresh, creamy sea urchin that melts in your mouth. It's a luxurious and unforgettable bite that showcases the peak of quality and preparation.

seeded_wataru.text: Wataru's omakase is an exquisite journey through the finest seasonal fish, meticulously sourced and expertly prepared by Chef Kotaro Kumita. Each nigiri piece is a tiny masterpiece, offering unparalleled freshness and nuanced flavors.

seeded_mashiko.text: Mashiko's omakase offers a unique and delicious experience with a strong commitment to sustainable seafood sourcing. The chefs expertly craft each piece, highlighting the natural flavors of responsibly caught fish.

seeded_japonessa.text: The Seattle Roll at Japonessa is a local favorite, combining spicy tuna, crab, and avocado, topped with seared salmon and a special sauce. It's a vibrant and flavorful fusion roll that perfectly captures the city's modern sushi scene.

seeded_maneki_restaurant.text: Maneki's Futomaki is a nostalgic taste of Seattle's sushi history, a generously sized roll packed with traditional ingredients like tamago, kampyo, and various vegetables. It's a comforting and authentic choice from one of the city's oldest Japanese restaurants.

seeded_umi_sake_house.text: The Crunchy Roll at Umi Sake House is a textural delight, featuring shrimp tempura, avocado, and cucumber, topped with crispy tempura flakes and a sweet sauce. It's a crowd-pleaser that perfectly blends savory and crunchy elements.

seeded_musashi_s.text: Musashi's Deluxe Nigiri Set offers incredible value without compromising on freshness or quality, making high-quality sushi accessible to everyone. The fish is consistently fresh and expertly cut, providing a satisfying and authentic sushi experience.

seeded_i_love_sushi_lake_union.text: The Dragon Roll at I Love Sushi on Lake Union is a beautifully presented and delicious classic, featuring unagi, avocado, and cucumber, often topped with more avocado and a sweet unagi sauce. It's a consistently satisfying choice known for its rich flavors and appealing presentation.

seeded_by_tae.text: By Tae's Handroll Set offers a delightful and interactive sushi experience, featuring expertly prepared ingredients wrapped in crisp nori for immediate enjoyment. Each handroll bursts with fresh flavors and perfect textures, making it a highly sought-after treat.

seeded_tacos_chukis.text: These iconic street-style tacos feature perfectly marinated pork, served with a signature slice of pineapple and a sprinkle of cheese. The combination of sweet, savory, and tangy flavors makes them an absolute must-try in Seattle.

seeded_marination_ma_kai.text: Marination's Kalua Pork Tacos are a Seattle institution, offering tender, smoky Hawaiian-style pulled pork topped with a vibrant tangy slaw. Enjoying these flavorful tacos with the stunning waterfront view is an unforgettable experience.

seeded_birrieria_tijuana.text: These deeply flavorful birria tacos are a local sensation, featuring slow-cooked, tender beef stuffed into cheese-griddled tortillas and served with rich consommé for dipping. Each bite is an explosion of savory, cheesy goodness that keeps people coming back.

seeded_carmelo_s_tacos.text: Carmelo's Suadero Tacos are renowned for their incredibly tender, slow-cooked beef, offering an authentic taste of Mexico City street food. The perfectly rendered meat, simple toppings, and fresh tortillas create a truly sublime taco experience.

seeded_el_camion.text: El Camion's Al Pastor tacos are a consistent crowd-pleaser, featuring thinly sliced, marinated pork cooked on a vertical spit and served with fresh pineapple. Their commitment to traditional preparation results in a perfectly balanced and flavorful taco.

seeded_asadero_sinaloa.text: For a premium taco experience, Asadero Sinaloa's Carne Asada Tacos are unparalleled, showcasing high-quality, perfectly grilled Sonoran-style steak. The smoky char and tender texture of the meat truly elevate this classic dish.

seeded_la_carta_de_oaxaca.text: La Carta de Oaxaca brings the vibrant flavors of Yucatán to Seattle with their Cochinita Pibil Tacos, featuring slow-roasted pork marinated in achiote and sour orange. The tender, citrusy pork is a delightful departure from typical taco offerings.

seeded_rancho_bravo_tacos.text: Rancho Bravo's Carnitas Tacos are a beloved staple, offering perfectly crispy yet tender slow-cooked pork that's rich in flavor. These no-frills tacos are consistently delicious and a go-to for authentic street-style carnitas.

seeded_pablo_y_pablo.text: Pablo y Pablo's Baja Fish Tacos are a fresh and vibrant option, featuring perfectly crispy fried cod, crunchy cabbage, and a zesty crema. They offer a delightful textural contrast and a bright, satisfying flavor profile.

seeded_dick_s_drive_in.text: A Seattle institution, the Dick's Deluxe is a simple, no-frills classic that has delighted generations with its perfectly grilled patty, special sauce, and fresh toppings. It's the quintessential late-night or quick-lunch burger, embodying Seattle's casual charm and delicious history.

seeded_red_mill_burgers.text: Known for its generous portions and high-quality ingredients, the Red Mill Deluxe is a gloriously messy and incredibly satisfying burger experience. The crispy bacon, proprietary Mill Sauce, and perfectly cooked patty make it a legendary indulgence that's worth the wait.

seeded_loretta_s_northwesterner.text: This unassuming dive bar burger is a masterclass in simplicity, featuring a perfectly smashed patty, melted American cheese, and classic fixings on a soft bun. It's a no-frills, incredibly juicy, and perfectly balanced burger that locals consistently rave about as one of the city's best.

seeded_katsu_burger.text: A unique and flavorful twist on the classic, the Godzilla Attack Burger features a crispy panko-fried beef patty, spicy mayo, and tonkatsu sauce. This Japanese-inspired creation offers an explosion of textures and tastes, making it a standout choice for adventurous burger lovers.

seeded_8oz_burger_co.text: The namesake burger at 8oz Burger & Co. showcases premium ingredients, including a perfectly cooked grass-fed patty, Beecher's Flagship cheddar, and house-made sauces. It's a sophisticated and incredibly flavorful gourmet burger that elevates the classic to an art form.

seeded_uneeda_burger.text: Uneeda Burger's Classic is a testament to quality ingredients and expert preparation, featuring a perfectly grilled patty, fresh lettuce, tomato, onion, and their signature sauce. It's a consistently delicious and satisfying burger that hits all the right notes for a well-crafted meal.

seeded_lil_woody_s.text: The Woody's Burger is a beloved staple, known for its juicy beef patty, Tillamook cheddar, house-made pickles, and special Lil Woody's Sauce. It's a perfectly balanced and consistently delicious burger that embodies the restaurant's fun and approachable vibe.

seeded_dahlia_bakery.text: A sophisticated take on the classic, The Dahlia Burger from Tom Douglas's iconic establishment features a high-quality patty, aged cheddar, and house-made pickles on a brioche bun. It's a consistently excellent and refined burger, perfect for a more upscale yet comforting dining experience.

seeded_burgermaster.text: A true Seattle classic, the Burgermaster Cheeseburger offers a nostalgic drive-in experience with its simple, perfectly grilled patty, melted cheese, and fresh toppings. It's a consistently satisfying and unpretentious burger that has been a local favorite for decades.

seeded_delancey.text: Delancey's Margherita is a masterclass in simplicity, featuring a perfectly charred crust, bright tomato sauce, fresh mozzarella, and fragrant basil. It's a testament to quality ingredients and expert execution, making every bite a sublime experience.

seeded_serious_pie.text: This pizza is a gourmet delight, boasting an earthy richness from roasted mushrooms and the luxurious aroma of truffle cheese. The thin, crispy crust provides the perfect canvas for these deep, savory flavors that truly sing.

seeded_pagliacci_pizza.text: A true Seattle institution, The Original delivers classic comfort with its perfectly balanced blend of pepperoni and mushrooms. It's a consistently satisfying choice that locals have loved for decades, perfect for any casual pizza night.

seeded_tutta_bella_neapolitan_pizzeria.text: Tutta Bella's Margherita D.O.C. adheres strictly to Neapolitan tradition, featuring San Marzano tomatoes, fresh mozzarella, and a beautifully blistered crust from their wood-fired oven. This authentic pie transports you straight to Naples with its vibrant, fresh flavors.

seeded_bar_del_corso.text: The Salsiccia pizza at Bar del Corso is a standout, showcasing their incredible house-made sausage with a perfect balance of spice and fennel. Paired with a light tomato sauce and fresh mozzarella on a chewy, charred crust, it's a truly memorable pie.

seeded_dino_s_tomato_pie.text: Dino's The OG is the quintessential Detroit-style pizza, featuring a thick, airy crust with crispy cheese edges and a vibrant red sauce on top. It's a deeply satisfying and unique pizza experience that's become a beloved local favorite.

seeded_moto_pizza.text: MOTO's Crab Pizza is an adventurous and unforgettable creation, loaded with sweet crab meat, garlic, and a creamy sauce, all on their signature deep-dish crust. This innovative pie is a must-try for anyone seeking a truly unique and decadent pizza experience in Seattle.

seeded_pizzeria_22.text: For those who love a bit of heat, Pizzeria 22's Diavola delivers with spicy salami and a perfectly blistered crust. The fiery kick is balanced by the fresh mozzarella and tomato, creating a robust and exciting flavor profile.

seeded_ballard_pizza_company.text: The Ballard is a beloved neighborhood staple, generously topped with classic pepperoni, sausage, mushrooms, and olives on a satisfyingly chewy crust. It's a reliable and delicious choice that embodies the spirit of a great New York-style slice.

seeded_rococo_pizza.text: Rococo Pizza's classic Pepperoni pie is a masterclass in New York-style simplicity, featuring perfectly cupped, crispy pepperoni and a fantastic cheese pull. The thin, foldable crust and savory toppings make it an incredibly satisfying and authentic slice.

seeded_spinasse.text: The delicate, impossibly thin strands of house-made tajarin are perfectly coated in a rich, nutty brown butter and sage sauce. It's a simple dish executed with such precision and flavor that it feels truly transcendent.

seeded_tavol_ta.text: This signature dish delivers a perfect kick of heat balanced with garlic, chili, and a hint of mint, all clinging to perfectly al dente spaghetti. It's a bold, satisfying plate that keeps locals coming back for more.

seeded_il_corvo_pasta.text: While their menu rotates daily, Il Corvo's rich, slow-cooked ragu dishes, often served with beautifully textured mafalde or other fresh pasta, are consistently outstanding. The pasta itself is a revelation, perfectly al dente and made with incredible care.

seeded_artusi.text: Artusi's rendition of this Roman classic is a masterclass in simplicity, featuring perfectly emulsified Pecorino Romano and black pepper sauce clinging to al dente pasta. It's a comforting and deeply flavorful dish that highlights the quality of its few ingredients.

seeded_cafe_lago.text: Cafe Lago's lasagne is a beloved institution, featuring delicate house-made pasta layered with a rich bechamel, savory meat ragu, and generous amounts of cheese. It's a hearty, comforting dish that embodies classic Italian home cooking.

seeded_la_medusa.text: This Sicilian staple shines with perfectly cooked bucatini tossed in a vibrant tomato sauce with tender eggplant, basil, and salty ricotta salata. It's a beautifully balanced and deeply satisfying vegetarian option.

seeded_san_fermo.text: San Fermo's Bolognese is a comforting and rich classic, featuring slow-simmered meat ragu clinging to perfectly textured house-made tagliatelle. It's a hearty and authentic dish that transports you straight to Italy.

seeded_pasta_casalinga.text: Pasta Casalinga delivers a truly authentic Carbonara, with perfectly emulsified egg, Pecorino Romano, crispy guanciale, and black pepper coating fresh, al dente spaghetti. It's a rich, creamy, and deeply satisfying Roman classic done right.

seeded_the_pink_door.text: The Pink Door's wild boar ragu is a deeply flavorful and rustic dish, with tender, slow-cooked meat clinging to broad, house-made pappardelle noodles. It's a rich and hearty option that perfectly complements the restaurant's unique ambiance.

seeded_mondello_ristorante.text: Mondello's Penne al Salmone is a beloved neighborhood favorite, featuring tender salmon pieces in a creamy tomato sauce with a hint of vodka, tossed with al dente penne. It's a comforting and surprisingly elegant dish that locals adore.

seeded_pho_bac_sup_shop.text: This iconic dish from Pho Bac Sup Shop offers a deeply flavorful and aromatic broth, a true benchmark for Seattle pho. It's a classic, comforting bowl that perfectly balances all the traditional elements.

seeded_dong_thap_noodles.dishName: Pho Dac Biet with Handmade Noodles
seeded_dong_thap_noodles.text: Famous for its incredibly fresh, chewy handmade noodles, this pho provides a unique textural experience unlike any other. The robust broth and generous portions make for an unforgettable and hearty meal.

seeded_pho_than_brothers.text: A Seattle institution, Than Brothers consistently delivers a clean, aromatic broth with perfectly tender rare steak. It's a reliable and comforting choice for a classic, no-frills pho experience loved by many.

seeded_green_leaf_vietnamese_restaurant.text: While known for other dishes, their Pho Ga stands out for its exceptionally clear, sweet chicken broth and tender shredded chicken. It offers a lighter yet equally satisfying alternative to traditional beef pho.

seeded_pho_cyclo_cafe.text: Pho Cyclo's broth is consistently praised for its depth and balance, hitting all the right savory notes. The variety of textures from the different cuts of meat makes each spoonful interesting and satisfying.

seeded_ba_bar.text: Ba Bar elevates traditional pho with high-quality ingredients and a refined broth that feels both authentic and gourmet. Their springy, flavorful beef meatballs are a highlight, adding a delightful chewiness to the rich broth.

seeded_pho_viet_anh.dishName: Pho Tai Gan (Rare Steak and Tendon Pho)
seeded_pho_viet_anh.text: A beloved neighborhood spot, Pho Viet Anh is known for its consistently flavorful and well-balanced broth. The tender rare steak combined with the gelatinous tendon creates a delightful textural contrast that keeps people coming back.

seeded_huong_binh.text: As a long-standing International District institution, Huong Binh serves a comforting and traditional pho with a deeply savory broth. It's a taste of classic Vietnamese home cooking, consistently satisfying and authentic.

seeded_pho_99.dishName: Pho Tai Chin (Rare Steak and Brisket Pho)
seeded_pho_99.text: Pho 99 is celebrated for its incredibly fragrant and clear broth, which is simmered to perfection. The combination of tender rare steak and melt-in-your-mouth brisket offers a hearty and satisfying experience.

seeded_jade_garden.text: These translucent dumplings are packed with perfectly cooked, sweet shrimp, offering a delightful snap with every bite. The delicate wrapper holds the succulent filling beautifully, making them a benchmark for quality.

seeded_jade_garden.dishName: Char Siu Bao (Steamed BBQ Pork Buns)
seeded_jade_garden.text: The fluffy, cloud-like buns at Jade Garden are legendary, encasing a generous portion of sweet and savory barbecued pork. Each bite is a perfect balance of soft dough and rich, flavorful filling.
A vibrant yellow wrapper encases a savory blend of seasoned pork and shrimp, often topped with a touch of roe. They are consistently juicy and flavorful, a quintessential dim sum experience.

Jade Garden's turnip cake is pan-fried to a beautiful crisp on the outside while remaining tender and savory within, studded with dried shrimp and Chinese sausage. It's a comforting and flavorful classic.

seeded_harbor_city_restaurant.text: Harbor City's cheung fun boasts incredibly smooth, thin rice noodles that melt in your mouth, wrapped around plump, fresh shrimp. Drizzled with a light soy sauce, it's a delicate and satisfying dish.

For the adventurous palate, these chicken feet are braised to tender perfection, falling off the bone with a rich, savory, and slightly spicy sauce. It's a deeply flavorful and authentic dim sum delicacy.
The egg tarts here feature a wonderfully flaky pastry crust filled with a silky-smooth, sweet custard that's not overly sugary. They make for a delightful sweet ending to any dim sum feast.

seeded_honey_court_seafood_restaurant.dishName: Lo Mai Gai (Sticky Rice in Lotus Leaf)
seeded_honey_court_seafood_restaurant.text: This hearty dish features fragrant glutinous rice steamed with savory chicken, Chinese sausage, and mushrooms, all wrapped in a lotus leaf. The aroma and rich flavors infuse every grain of rice, making it incredibly satisfying.

seeded_dough_zone_dumpling_house.text: Dough Zone's XLB are a true masterpiece, with delicate wrappers holding a rich, savory pork filling and an explosion of hot, flavorful broth. Each dumpling is a perfect balance of texture and taste, a must-try for any dumpling lover.

These pan-fried buns offer an incredible textural contrast: a crispy, golden-brown bottom, fluffy steamed top, and a juicy pork filling with a burst of soup. They are incredibly satisfying and addictive.

seeded_thai_tom.text: Thai Tom's Pad Thai is legendary for its intense wok hei, perfectly chewy noodles, and a harmonious balance of sweet, sour, and savory flavors. It's a quintessential Seattle experience, offering an authentic taste of street-style Thai cuisine that keeps locals coming back for more.

seeded_may_kitchen_bar.text: While a scenic ferry ride away on Vashon Island, May Kitchen + Bar's Pad Thai is a destination dish, celebrated for its elegant presentation and deeply nuanced flavors. Made with high-quality, fresh ingredients, the sauce is a delicate masterpiece that truly elevates the classic dish.

seeded_kin_len_thai_night_bites.text: Kin Len's Pad Thai offers a vibrant and authentic street-food experience, known for its perfectly cooked noodles and a sauce that strikes a delightful balance. It's praised for its fresh, bold flavors that transport you straight to the bustling night markets of Thailand.

seeded_remarkable_thai.text: This restaurant lives up to its name with a consistently excellent Pad Thai that is both comforting and remarkably flavorful. The dish is known for its well-balanced sauce that avoids being overly sweet, making it a reliable choice for a satisfying meal.

seeded_racha_noodle_thai_cuisine.text: Racha's Pad Thai is a beloved staple, offering a comforting and flavorful rendition that consistently hits the mark. The noodles are always perfectly cooked, and the sauce is a delightful blend of traditional sweet, sour, and savory notes.

seeded_aroy_seattle.text: Aroy Seattle is celebrated for its commitment to authentic Thai flavors, and their Pad Thai is a testament to this dedication. It boasts a deep, complex sauce and fresh ingredients that truly capture the essence of traditional Thai cooking.

seeded_manao_thai_street_eats.text: True to its name, Manao delivers a vibrant and robust Street Style Pad Thai that perfectly encapsulates the energy of Thai street food. The dish is known for its generous portions and an intensely flavorful profile that satisfies every craving.

seeded_noodle_boat.text: Noodle Boat is a local favorite for its consistent quality and generous portions, and their Pad Thai is a standout. It offers a perfect balance of sweet, sour, and savory, with perfectly cooked noodles and fresh ingredients.

seeded_pattaya_restaurant_bar.text: A long-standing establishment, Pattaya's Pad Thai is a comforting and reliable choice, known for its classic preparation and well-balanced flavors. It's a beloved neighborhood staple that consistently delivers a satisfying meal.

seeded_bangkok_garden.text: Bangkok Garden offers a time-tested Pad Thai recipe that has delighted Seattle diners for years. Its rich, tangy, and slightly sweet sauce perfectly coats the tender noodles and fresh shrimp, making it an consistently enjoyable dish.

seeded_voodoo_donuts.text: Voodoo Donuts is iconic for a reason. The Grand Opening Special — a chocolate doughnut with maple frosting, bacon, and Oreos — is an absolute flavor rollercoaster. Sweet, salty, savory, and completely unapologetic about it. Portland's food culture in one bite.

The signature Voodoo Doll — a sugar doughnut with chocolate frosting and a pretzel stake — is as photogenic as it is tasty. The cream filling is rich and not overly sweet. The novelty is real but the dough is genuinely good.

seeded_pine_street_market.text: Pine Street Market's Hearthside Burger is a revelation — wagyu beef, aged cheddar, caramelized onions, and a secret sauce on a house-baked bun. The char from the open hearth gives it an incredible smoky depth. Best burger in Portland, hands down.

The Affogado — vanilla gelato with a shot of espresso and amaretto — is the perfect end to a Portland food hall crawl. Rich, cold, and just the right amount of bitter. Share one or don't. No judgment.

seeded_tasty_n_daughters.text: Tasty n Daughters serves the best shakshuka in the Pacific Northwest. The tomato-pepper sauce is deeply spiced and slightly sweet, poached eggs are runny perfection, and the sourdough for dipping is made in-house. Sunday brunch here is a religious experience.

Their fried chicken is exceptional — crispy, well-seasoned, and juicy. The honey butter drizzled over it is a nice touch. Served with collard greens and jalapeño cornbread. It's hearty, Southern-inspired comfort food done right.

seeded_podnahs_pit.text: Podnah's Pit is the real deal for Texas-style BBQ in Portland. The brisket is smoked for 14+ hours until it practically melts — bark that snaps, fat cap that's rendering into something magical. Served with two sauces: a classic tomato-based and a tangier vinegar number.

The pulled pork is tender, smoke-kissed, and topped with tangy coleslaw on a brioche bun. The housemade pickles add crunch and acidity. It's a no-frills, just-done-right kind of sandwich. Feed a hungry crowd or eat two yourself.

seeded_nongs_khao_man_gai.text: Nong's Khao Man Gai is legendary for good reason. The chicken rice — poached chicken over fragrant garlic-cilantro rice with a Side of tamarind sauce — is deceptively simple but impossibly good. The tamarind sauce is the secret weapon. Portland's most beloved food cart turned brick-and-mortar.

Her Khao Soi is a rich, coconut curry noodle soup with tender chicken and a crispy noodle topping. The balance of sweet, savory, and earthy spices is masterful. One bowl and you'll understand why people line up at the food cart.

seeded_tiffin_asha.text: Tiffin Asha puts a creative Indo-Canadian spin on classics. The butter chicken here has a richer, more complex spice profile than most — cardamom, fenugreek, and a hint of something smoky. Served with fluffy basmati and fresh naan. Comfort food with personality.

Masala fries are the ultimate fusion snack — crispy fries loaded with spiced chickpeas, tamarind chutney, yogurt sauce, and fresh cilantro. It's chaat meets poutine. Unexpectedly addictive and exactly what Portland food scene should be.

seeded_grassa_pdx.text: Grassa does pasta the right way — handmade in-house, cooked precisely, sauced simply. The cacio e pepe is a masterclass in restraint: just pasta, pecorino, black pepper. The peppery bite cuts through the rich cheese. It's Italian done with serious respect.

The Bolognese here simmers for hours, building deep meaty flavor. Served with wide pappardelle that holds the sauce perfectly. Comfort food elevated — the kind of dish that makes you want to cancel your dinner reservations and stay for seconds.

seeded_ox_restaurant.text: Ox is Portland's best Argentinian grill experience. The asado — slow-grilled beef short ribs and flank steak over wood fire — has an incredible char and smoky depth. The chimichurri is bright and herbaceous. It's meat done right, with fire and patience.

The empanadas at Ox are perfectly fried — flaky dough, seasoned beef filling, served with a spicy green sauce. They're the ideal starter before the main event. Get two or three to share, or don't share at all.

seeded_apizza_scholls.text: Apizza Scholls makes Detroit-style pizza the way it should be — thick, chewy crust with crispy edges, mozzarella layered right to the edge, sauce on top, and housemade Italian sausage. The pepperoni cups up perfectly. This is the best pizza in Portland, no contest.

The bianca — white pizza with ricotta, mozzarella, garlic, and olive oil — is for people who think pizza doesn't need tomato. The ricotta is creamy, the garlic is mellow, and the crust still delivers that signature Scholls chew. A welcome deviation from the classic.

seeded_kenjis_pdx.text: Kenji's fried chicken over rice with soft egg, scallions, and spicy mayo is the ultimate umami bomb. The chicken is shatteringly crispy, the egg yolk runs into the rice creating a rich sauce, and the spicy mayo brings just enough heat. Fast-casual done at an extremely high level.

The karé kare — oxtail stew with peanut sauce, eggplant, and bok choy — is rich, deeply flavored, and comes with a side of bagoong for added funk. It's a lesser-known Filipino classic done with care. Comforting in a completely different way than American food.

seeded_truffle_pdx.text: Truffle's truffle fries are the gold standard — hand-cut, perfectly crispy, tossed in truffle oil, parmesan, and fresh herbs. They come with an aioli that's garlicky and bright. Worth the calories and then some.

The braised short rib is fork-tender, falling apart in the most satisfying way. Served with creamy polenta and a red wine reduction. It's the kind of dish you'd find at a high-end Montana lodge — hearty, refined, and deeply comforting.

seeded_gjusta.text: Gjusta's smoked salmon is silky, perfectly salted, and paired with excellent sourdough and house-made schmears. The space is gorgeous — part bakery, part café, all Venice Beach institution. Come early; they sell out.
The avo toast at Gjusta is a masterclass in simplicity — thick-cut sourdough, smashed avo,house-pickled onions, and sesame. Simple but executed at the highest level. Great with a latte.

seeded_repeta.text: Repeta's croissant is what all croissants aspire to be — golden, flaky, buttery with a shattering crust and pillowy interior. Get there before 10am. Cash only, lines move fast.
The kouign-amann is caramelized perfection — crispy, caramelized exterior giving way to a soft, buttery center. It's almost too beautiful to eat. Almost.

seeded_marisol_midcity.text: Marisol's grilled octopus is tender with a perfect char, served over white bean purée and finished with a bright Meyer lemon dressing. Refined but not fussy. One of the best in LA.
The wood-roasted chicken is juicy, crispy-skinned, and comes with excellent roasted vegetables. Simple, satisfying, and done right. Great for sharing.

seeded_hatchet_hall.text: Hatchet Hall's 32-day dry-aged ribeye is a carnivore's dream — deeply flavored, buttery-tender, and kissed with smoke from the wood fire. Accompanied by an excellent raw bar. Reserve the chef's counter.
The FCS is a weekend legend — crispy thigh, house pickles, spicy honey, on a brioche bun. Messy, addictive, and worth the wait. Wash it down with a mezcal cocktail.

seeded_ URIU.text: URIU's omakase is a highlight of the Westside — impeccably sourced fish, generous cuts, andHideaki San's warm hospitality. The hamachi is transcendent. Pricey but worth every penny for a special occasion.
Silky, savory egg custard with seasonal toppings. A delicate start to the omakase that showcases Japanese precision at its finest.

seeded_fat_niece_creamery.text: Fat Chance's almond brulee is legendary — house-made almond milk-based ice cream with a torched caramelized sugar crust. The crunch, the creaminess, the toasted almond flavor — perfection.
Seasonal strawberry ice cream with macarons. Light, fruity, and surprisingly refreshing. Only available when strawberries are in season — follow them on Instagram to know when.

seeded_phreak_bbq.text: Phreak Barbecue's brisket is Texas-style done LA proud — deep smoke ring, peppery bark, and a juice factor that's off the charts. Trimmed and wrapped to specification. Pick it up on Saturdays only.
The pulled pork is a pleasant surprise — slow-cooked, porky, with a tangy Carolina-style vinegar slaw. Less famous than the brisket but arguably more exciting.

seeded_jua.text: Jua's Korean-influenced omakase is unlike anything else in LA — DJ in the kitchen, family-style spreads, and dishes like bosari uni congee and fried chicken with gochujang. It's a whole experience, not just dinner.
The braised short rib is Korea meets France — fall-apart tender, rich, with a depth that comes from 48-hour sous vide. Served with lotus rice and kimchi that actually ferments properly.

seeded_woodcat_cafe.text: Woodcat's brown butter latte is caffeine and comfort in a cup — nutty, slightly sweet, with excellent espresso. The café itself is gorgeous — Japanese-farmhouse vibes, very zen.
White miso butter on thick-cut sourdough with seasonal toppings. Sweet, salty, umami. Simple concept, executed beautifully. Pairs perfectly with any of their specialty drinks.

seeded_oda_isla.text: Oda Isla's ceviche is bright, citrus-forward, and made with incredibly fresh fish flown in regularly. The clásico is perfection, but the leche de tigre is the real star — spicy, sour, and totally addictive.
Tuna tostada with aguachile, serrano, and micro cilantro. The balance of spice, acid, and umami is spot-on. The space is tiny — counter seating only, 86'd by 2pm most days.

seeded_tartine_bakery.text: Tartine's country loaf is the benchmark for San Francisco sourdough — crackling crust, open crumb, with a tangy, complex flavor. Arrive by 8am or join the line. Worth every minute of waiting.
The morning bun is SF street food royalty — croissant dough rolled in orange sugar and cinnamon, baked fresh. Crispy, flaky, with a burst of citrusy sweetness. Dangerously addictive.
Seasonal lobster roll with tarragon aioli on a buttered brioche roll. Generous chunks of sweet lobster, dressed just enough. Only available in summer — follows their seasonal menu.

seeded_swan_offering.text: Swan Offering's roast duck is the best in the Bay Area — crisp skin, perfectly rendered fat, and meat that pulls apart like butter. Chinese roast techniques perfected over decades. Get the half duck.
The Peking duck is a multi-course experience — first the crispy skin with pancakes, then the meat stir-fried with vegetables. Expensive but unforgettable. Call ahead.

seeded_liholiho_yacht_club.text: Liholiho's Cantonese lobster is legendary — buttery lobster tail, egg fried rice, and heads-on prawns in a clay pot. Messy, pungent, totally addictive. One of those dishes that lives in your head for years.
The miso wings are a UMAMI BOMB — deep-fried, sauced, topped with bonito. Messy, sticky, and completely irreplaceable. One of the best wings in the city, SF or otherwise.
Braised oxtail stirred into egg fried rice with a fried egg on top. Rich, unctuous, with the kind of depth that comes from slow braising. Messy to eat, impossible to stop.

seeded_artesiano.text: Artesiano's squid ink paella is jet black, dramatic, and deeply savory — cuttlefish ink giving it an intense seafood depth. The socarrat is perfectly crispy. One of the best paellas outside of Spain.
Grilled octopus with fingerling potatoes, smoked paprika, and olive oil. The octopus is tender with a nice char, the potatoes add substance. Solid, satisfying, and consistently excellent.

seeded_zuni_cafe.text: Zuni's legendary roast chicken is a San Francisco institution — salt-cured, brick-roasted, served with a phenomenal bread salad and bitter greens. The chicken is juicy, the skin is crisp, the drippings are magical.
The Caesar at Zuni is classic and beloved — Romesco, anchovy, good Parmesan. Sometimes over-marinated but when it's on, it's on. The Caesar is the salad against which all others are measured.

seeded_starbelly.text: Starbelly's smoked trout dip is creamy, smoky, and comes with excellent house chips. A perfect bar snack. The space is fun, the cocktails are solid, and the trout dip keeps you coming back.
Mussels in a white wine, garlic, and herb broth with grilled bread. Simple, perfectly executed, and the broth is so good you'll want to drink it with a spoon.

seeded_the_burger.text: Richie Seban's burger is SF's best — 8oz dry-aged wagyu, American cheese, house pickles, special sauce, on a brioche bun. The patty is umami-rich and the jus makes it messily excellent.
Dry-aged ribeye with frites and béarnaise. Simple, French-bistro perfect. The frites are thin and crispy, the béarnaise is silky. Not fancy, but everything you want.

seeded_calabash.text: Calabash's jambalaya is the real deal — deeply flavored, smoky, with the holy trinity and andouille sausage. One pot, soul-satisfying. Feeds two easily. Order ahead, it's made in batches.
Crispy, cornmeal-crusted catfish with creole remoulade. The fish is fresh, the coating is perfect, and the remoulade adds just the right amount of zip. A New Orleans moment in SF.

seeded_hog_oyster.text: Hog & Oyster's fried chicken is legendary — ultra-crispy, buttermilk-brined, with a side of oyster stew that seems like it shouldn't work but absolutely does. Southern cooking done with SF precision.
Creamy, briny, with a hint of sherry. A small cup that hits hard on a foggy San Francisco evening. Pairs perfectly with the fried chicken.

seeded_nopalito.text: Nopalito's pozole is a Saturday morning tradition for regulars — rich, deeply flavored hominy soup with pork, shredded cabbage, radish, oregano, and dried chili. Slow-cooked for hours, possibly days.
Birria lamb with consomé for dipping tortillas. The lamb is fall-apart tender, the consomé is rich and complex. Messy and satisfying. Get extra tortillas.

seeded_franklin_bbq.text: Franklin's brisket is the reason Texas BBQ exists — 16-hour smoke, perfect pepper bark, slice that melts on your tongue. The most famous BBQ in the world, and somehow still worth the wait. Bring a lawn chair.
Franklin's pulled pork doesn't get the glory but it should — pork shoulder, smoked low and slow, with a tangy vinegar slaw that cuts right through. My personal favorite.
House-made sausage links with a great snap and solid spice. The sausage gets overshadowed by the brisket but it's consistently excellent. Get a few links.

seeded_lamberts_downtown.text: Lambert's big beef ribs are a carnivore's dream — massive, meaty, with an incredible bark and smoke ring. The size alone is worth the Instagram. The flavor keeps you coming back.
Lambert's mac and cheese is properly baked — creamy, with a crunchy top, and plenty of cheese. A solid side that holds its own against the smoked meats.

seeded_vaULTS.text: VAULT's smoked half chicken is underrated — juicy, with a perfect bark and smoke. Not as famous as their links but arguably better. The jalapeño cheese sausage is also exceptional.
VAULT's jalapeño cheese sausage is a revelation — coarse grind, real cheese chunks, fresh jalapeño heat. Better than most dedicated sausage places. Get two.

seeded_st_elmo_brewery.text: St. Elmo's burnt ends are nuggets of beefy, barky, caramelized perfection. Small, precious, and aggressively smoky. One of the most sought-after items in Austin BBQ.
The turkey breast is moist, well-smoked, and comes with an excellent jalapeño凑 béarnaise. A lighter option that doesn't compromise on quality.

seeded_verdegreen.text: Verde's duck confit is French technique meets Texas ingredients — crispy skin, tender meat, on a bed of excellent greens with a sherry vinaigrette. Light, bright, and totally satisfying.
Braised short rib with green chile polenta and gremolata. Rich, hearty, with a New Mexico green chile kick. The polenta is creamy, the gremolata adds brightness.

seeded_pretty_cool_ice_cream.text: Pretty Cool's horchata ice cream is a Tex-Mex dream — cinnamon, rice milk, and just enough vanilla to tie it together. Silky, spiced, and incredibly refreshing. Some of the best ice cream in Austin.
Seasonal strawberry basil with local strawberries. Light, herby, not too sweet. The basil is subtle but present, making this more interesting than your average fruit ice cream.

seeded_uchiko_austin.text: Uchiko's omakase is the best in Austin — beautiful fish, thoughtful presentations, and a sake program that's actually interesting. Not cheap, but every course is a small celebration.
The miso-black cod is legendary for good reason — rich, buttery, with the sweet-salty miso glaze caramelized to perfection. One of the best preparations of this fish in the country.
Uchiko's fried chicken is unexpectedly excellent — karaage-style, with shiso aioli and house-made pickles. One of the best non-BBQ proteins in Austin.

seeded_contigo_ranch.text: Contigo Ranch's smoked beef cheek is an underdog favorite — deeply smoked, rich, and tender enough to cut with a fork. The quality of the beef really shines through.
Smoked wings with jalapeño honey and ranch. The smoke gives them depth that fried wings lack. Spicy, sweet, and messy. One of the best wings in Austin.

seeded_ la_barba.text: La Barba's smoked pastrami is a beautiful thing — house-brined, heavily peppered, and smoked until impossibly tender. Sliced thin, piled high. Way better than it needs to be.
The brisket Reuben with house-made Thousand Island and rye bread. A perfect use of leftover brisket. Heavy, messy, and completely satisfying.

seeded_jewboy_burgers.text: Jewboy's matzo ball is legendary — dense, savory, with a schmaltz kick. The soup itself is clear, rich, with excellent hand-rolled noodles. The ultimate Austin comfort food.
House-made pastrami on rye with sauerkraut and Russian dressing. The pastrami is juicy and well-spiced, the sauerkraut adds crunch, the dressing ties it together.

seeded_ivys_dumpling.text: Ivy's soup dumplings in Flushing are NYC's best — thin-skinned, broth-filled, and incredibly savory. Get the pork and crab, add the black vinegar. This is the reason people take the 7 train to Queens.
Hand-pulled noodles with scallion oil and soy. Simple, wok-hei, and deeply comforting. The kind of dish that makes you want to stay for another plate.

seeded_ha_pei.text: Haand's pepper steak is a must — rich, savory, with a creamy peppercorn sauce that's dangerously good. The quality of the beef is exceptional for a sit-down. Come hungry.
Crispy crab rangoon with house sweet chili sauce. More filling than expected, with real crab flavor. A solid appetizer that disappears fast.

seeded_crown_shy.text: Crown Shy's dry-aged ribeye is NYC fine dining done right — not pretentious, with a serious wine program and impeccable service. The beef is aged to perfection, the butter is house-made.
Potato agnolotti with brown butter and sage. Pillowy pasta, rich filling, nutty butter sauce. Simple Italian preparation at its absolute finest.

seeded_kang_ho_dong_ja.text: Kang Ho Dong's beef short rib is the flagship — marinated in soy, sesame, pear, and garlic, then grilled to caramelized perfection. A NYC institution for good reason.
Spicy pork neck stew with kimchi and glass noodles. The spice builds slowly, the pork neck is collagen-rich and tender. A great share dish for two.

seeded_noreet_thai.text: Noreet's Khao Soi is legendary in Chiang Mai style — rich coconut curry broth with egg noodles, chicken thigh, and crispy shallots. The fried shallots are addictive.
Green papaya salad with dried shrimp, peanuts, tomato, and a lime-chili dressing. Crunchy, spicy, sour. A perfect balance of Thai flavors.

seeded_avant_garden.text: Avant Garden's vegetable tasting is a revelation — every course proves that plants can be the star. The smoked carrot and romesco course is particularly memorable.
Silky wild mushroom soup with truffle oil and crispy shallots. Rich, earthy, and satisfying even for committed carnivores. A standout in the East Village.

seeded_katzs_deli.text: Katz's pastrami is a New York institution — hand-sliced, hot, with spicy mustard on rye. Get the full pastrami, not the Royale. The Ephron scene is real. Don't lose your ticket.
Katz's corned beef is salt-cured to perfection — thick-sliced, tender, with the classic caraway flavor. A solid alternative to the pastrami. Comes with a half-sour pickle.

seeded_russ_daughters.text: The Super Heebster is a bagel piled high with sturgeon, wasabi roe, horseradish cream cheese, and dill. A Lower East Side institution. Smoked fish at its most gloriously excess.
Classic nova lox with cream cheese on a bagel. The fish is silky, the cream cheese is thick, the bagel is properly toasted. Breakfast perfection.

seeded_fuku.text: Fuku's fried chicken sandwich is David Chang's masterpiece — crispy chicken, spicy mayo, and a brioche bun. Simple, addictive, and the spicy version has real heat.
Fuku fries with the duck fat seasoning. Crispy, salty, with a hint of funk from the duck fat. Add the spicy mayo for dipping and you won't stop until the container is empty.

seeded_pete_zaaz.text: Pete Zazu's za'atar fries are the best fries in the East Village — crispy, tossed in za'atar and sumac, with tahini and amba. Middle Eastern spice, zero pretense.
Lamb and chicken shawarma with hummus, pita, and amba. The meat is well-spiced, the hummus is silky, the amba adds mango tang. A satisfying, filling meal.

seeded_grams_ burger.text: Grams' burger is old-school done right — double smashed patties, American cheese, special sauce, on a potato bun. The patties are thin, crispy-edged, and mega savory.
Grilled cheese with American cheese and bread and butter pickles. Simple, nostalgic, with the perfect ratio of crispy bread to melted cheese. Worth the wait in line.
